chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI SCRA 340 of 2019 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objections No.8 & 9. 2. For orders on CMA No.3233/2019. 3. For hearing of main case. 4. For orders on CMA No.3234/2019. 02.04.2026 Mr. Mustafa Safvi, advocate for the applicant. Matter is pending since 2019 without any progress. On 16.08.2019 following order was passed: “16.08.2019 Mr. Mustafa Safvi advocate for applicant. 1. Granted. 2. At the request of learned counsel, two weeks’ time is granted for compliance.” On 12.03.2020, following order was passed: “12.03.2020 Mr. Amaad Hussain advocate hodling brief for Mr. Mustafa Safvi advocate for applicant, who is reportedly unwell and requests for adjournment. Adjourned.” On 27.01.2026, following order was passed: “27.01.2026 Mr. Mustafa Safvi, advocate for the applicant. At the request of learned counsel adjourned to 10.02.2026.” On 10.02.2026, following order was passed: “10.02.2026 Mr. Mustafa Safvi, advocate for the applicant. Once again learned counsel remains unable to articulate any question of law arising here from and requests for time. At his request adjourned to come up after six weeks.” Today once again learned counsel seeks time. It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969.

Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
